events.note: New inbox rule that auto-forwards mail to an external address and deletes the original — classic BEC exfil + hide pattern.
Mailbox-level forwarding to an external SMTP address — survives inbox-rule cleanup and forwards ALL mail silently.

mutation_hints.objective: Establish covert, persistent external mail exfiltration via inbox or mailbox forwarding
detection_anchors: Field match: Operation=New-InboxRule → change to Set-InboxRule (modifying an existing rule rather than creating a new one — many rules only catch 'New-' operations); or use UpdateInboxRules (Outlook client-side equivalent)

Wildcard match: Parameters contains ForwardTo → change to ForwardAsAttachmentTo (forwards as attachment — different parameter name that avoids 'ForwardTo' pattern) or RedirectTo (redirect vs forward — different field name)
Wildcard match: Parameters contains DeleteMessage=True → change to False (forward without deleting original — less obvious evasion; exfil still happens but the DeleteMessage trigger is gone)

Field match: Operation=Set-Mailbox AND Parameters contains ForwardingSmtpAddress → use DeliverToMailboxAndForward=True instead of False — mail still forwards externally but also stays in mailbox, making it look more like a legitimate forward-and-keep rule
Field: ClientIP → use a different cloud exit IP or an internal IP if the attacker is already inside the network — some rules anchor on known-bad external IPs
Field: Workload=Exchange → keep Exchange; changing to a different workload changes the operation type entirely
Field: RuleName → use a benign-looking name like 'Archive', 'Backup', or 'Newsletter Filter' instead of '.' (a single dot is itself an indicator of obfuscated rule naming)
